reforms.description: Industrial enterprises are allowed a taxation deduction for R&D expenditures for the years 1984-1988, which is contingent on both the level of and increase in the research wage and salary bill.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private sector investment in research and development, which can lead to innovation and increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Industrial enterprises are allowed R&D taxation deduction for years 1984-1988.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy shift aimed at stimulating R&D investment in the private sector, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Industrial enterprises allowed R&D taxation deduction for years 1984-1988, depending on both the level of, and increase in, research wage and salary bill.
